column1: # Human Ensembl v86, N=1000 stratified queries (first 1000 of queries_v86.bed),
# single machine, single thread. setup_s = one-time index/object build;
# map_s = mapping the 1000 queries; peak_rss_mb = VmHWM of the process.
# exact_match_pct = per-query exact genomic-segment agreement vs the others.

column8: C++ binary; setup = index load (v86). map_q/s from N=5000 (99ms); N=1000 was 18ms.
GRangesList method; setup = cdsBy(edb,'tx') + protein->tx map; in-memory mapping (R-level lapply).

column1: # VisProDom::CreDat — separate workload (its own maize proteome example data),
# not directly comparable on v86. Pure R/dplyr; O(genome) per call (rebuilds the
# full CDS layout every invocation), so wall is ~flat across domain count:
#   FULL (262,392 mapped domain-segments): 17.9 s, peak 780 MB
# geneplot — visualization only (gffutils + BioPython GenomeDiagram); consumes
# pre-mapped GFF3 + InterProScan coordinates and renders. Not a coordinate mapper.
